items.q: What is the difference between free and bound morphemes?
items.correct: Free morphemes can stand alone as words ('cat', 'happy', 'run'); bound morphemes must attach to another morpheme (prefixes, suffixes — 'un-', '-ness', '-ing').
items.misconception: Free morphemes are free to use and bound morphemes cost money to use.
items.random_wrong: Free morphemes are nouns and bound morphemes are verbs.
items.why: 'Free' and 'bound' refer to structural independence, not cost or word class. Students take 'free' literally or assign meaning by part of speech, missing the structural criterion.

items.q: What is Skinner's behaviourist view of language acquisition?
items.correct: Children learn language through imitation and operant conditioning — they are reinforced for correct utterances and corrected for errors.
items.misconception: Skinner believed in an innate grammar module.
items.random_wrong: Skinner believed children couldn't learn language until age five.
items.why: Skinner is the BEHAVIOURIST — the opposite of nativists like Chomsky. Students mix up the two. Skinner's view is environmental; Chomsky's is innate.

items.q: What is the difference between a dialect and an accent?
items.correct: Accent refers only to pronunciation; dialect includes vocabulary and grammar as well as pronunciation — so all dialects involve accents but not vice versa.
items.misconception: Dialect and accent mean the same thing.
items.random_wrong: A dialect is a foreign language; an accent is how locals speak.
items.why: The two terms are commonly conflated in everyday use. In linguistics, accent is a subset of dialect — dialect is broader, covering all variation, accent only sound.

items.q: A written record of speech is a.
items.why: The answer examiners want is: Transcript. “Sonnet” is the distractor this question was built around — if it tempted you, that's the exact gap to close.

items.q: Stress, tone and rhythm of speech are called.
items.why: Correct answer: Prosody. “Pause” sounds right because it borrows the language of the topic — but it fails this question. Re-read the notes on why.

items.q: How many transcript features named (pause, overlap, repair)?
items.why: Exact answer: 3. Estimation gets you near it; only the anchored number survives the exam hall.
